title: Fresh Canadian Halibut Nigiri
description: A simple yet elegant dish featuring succulent Canadian halibut, crunchy candied peel, and traditional nigiri-shaping. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Rinse the halibut fillets under cold water and pat dry with paper towels.
Cut the orange peel into thin slices and cook in a saucepan with the granulated sugar over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the peel is crispy and caramelized.
Prepare the nigiri by placing a small amount of cooked Japanese rice onto a flat surface, then gently shape into a small oval.
Place a halibut fillet on top of the rice and gently press down to adhere.
To make the candied orange peel, allow it to cool completely, then chop into small pieces.
To assemble the nigiri, place a small piece of candied orange peel on top of the halibut fillet.
Garnish with sesame seeds and salmon roe, if desired.
For the ginger sauce, grate the ginger and mix with soy sauce and ice-cold water in a small bowl.
Drizzle the ginger sauce over the nigiri and serve.
Serve the nigiri with additional ginger sauce on the side, if desired.
